text: There is a reception on the first floor.
There are three meeting rooms on the second floor.
Excuse me, can you help me?
I am responsible for large risks.
Schedule
Morning
Afternoon
Appointment
Deadline
Busy
The meeting is at nine thirty.
I visit the brokers on Tuesday.
We travel to Miami in November.
I have an appointment at three, on Friday.
Paula, I need thirty minutes with you this week. What time works for you?
Let me look at my schedule. I'm busy on Monday — the welcome meeting is at nine thirty, and I meet the team at two.
OK. On Tuesday, then?
On Tuesday I visit a broker with Sofia. We leave at eight, in the morning.
But you come back in the afternoon, right? Are you free at four?
I have an appointment at two, but four is free. Tuesday, at four?
Tuesday at four. And your report — the deadline is on Friday, at five. Don't forget.
On Friday at five. It's in my schedule.
Then we're good. See you on Tuesday, Paula.
What time is the meeting?
I have a call at ten, on Monday.
I'm busy on Monday. Are you free on Tuesday?
The deadline is on Friday, at five.
My appointment is in the afternoon.

Hi, Paula. David here. Your first week is in your schedule now, but let me tell you the important parts. On Monday you start at nine, and the welcome meeting is at nine thirty. Sofia is busy in the morning — she reads the reports — so she comes at two, in the afternoon. On Tuesday you visit a broker with her. You leave at eight, early. You come back in the afternoon, and I book us for four. And the deadline: your first report goes to Sofia on Friday, at five. Not on Monday. One more thing, and this is important: if your schedule is full, say it. "I'm busy" is a normal answer here. Nobody is angry. See you on Monday, at nine.

Good morning, Paula. Sofia here, about your week and about mine. My schedule is open — look at it, and book me. If I am busy, book the next day; I am not going anywhere. In the morning I read reports, so book me in the afternoon — after two is better. Now, the big one: in November we travel to Miami. That is far, and it is not your problem this week. Your problem this week is Friday, at five: the deadline for your first report. Small things first. And Paula, one more thing. In twenty years, nobody made a good report at midnight. Send it on Friday at five, and go home. See you on Monday.
